questions.question: What is macro photography?
options: Close-up photography that captures small subjects at life-size or greater magnification
questions.reviewHint: Macro is extreme close-up work, reproducing small subjects at life-size (1:1) or larger.

questions.question: A true macro lens offers a reproduction ratio of at least…
questions.reviewHint: A 1:1 ratio means the subject is projected onto the sensor at its real-life size.

questions.question: A defining challenge of macro photography is that…
options: Depth of field becomes extremely shallow
questions.reviewHint: At close focusing distances, depth of field collapses to millimetres.

questions.question: To increase depth of field in macro, you typically…
options: Stop down to a smaller aperture (higher f-number)
Use a faster shutter speed only
questions.reviewHint: Smaller apertures extend the very shallow macro depth of field.

questions.question: A side effect of using very small apertures in macro is…
questions.reviewHint: Stopping down too far introduces diffraction softening - a balance is needed.

questions.question: 'Working distance' in macro refers to…
options: The distance between the front of the lens and the subject
The distance you stand from the tripod
The focal length of the lens
questions.reviewHint: Working distance is lens-front to subject; longer working distance helps avoid disturbing live subjects.

questions.question: Focus stacking is used in macro to…
options: Combine multiple images focused at different points for greater sharpness front-to-back
questions.reviewHint: Stacking blends several frames at different focus points to overcome shallow macro depth of field.

questions.question: Why is stable support (e.g. a tripod) especially important in macro?
options: Tiny movements are magnified, so even slight shake ruins sharpness
It is only needed in daylight
questions.reviewHint: At high magnification the smallest movement is exaggerated, so stability is critical.

questions.question: Lighting is often challenging in macro because…
options: The lens or camera can block or shade the small subject
Macro subjects are always too bright
Light has no effect at close range
questions.reviewHint: Being so close, the gear can cast shadows; diffused or dedicated macro lighting helps.

questions.question: Manual focus is often preferred in macro because…
options: It gives precise control over the very thin plane of focus
questions.reviewHint: With a wafer-thin focus plane, manual focus lets you place sharpness exactly.

questions.question: Which accessory can let a normal lens focus closer for macro-style shots?
questions.reviewHint: Extension tubes move the lens further from the sensor, allowing closer focusing.

questions.question: A good aperture starting point for many macro subjects is around…
options: f/8 to f/16, balancing depth of field against diffraction
questions.reviewHint: Mid-to-small apertures (≈f/8-f/16) balance depth of field with acceptable sharpness.
